---
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    format-links: false
    css: styles.css
    body-classes: cv-page
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: "left=2cm,right=2cm,top=2cm,bottom=2cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{12pt}{8pt}
---

## Education

**MPH — Environmental Epidemiology**\
*Georgia State University · 2024–2026*

**LEND^[Leadership Education in Neurodevelopmental and Related Disabilities] Trainee**\
*Georgia State University · Aug 2025–Apr 2026*

**BS — History, Technology and Society**\
*Georgia Institute of Technology · 2019–2022*

## Professional & Research Experience

**Enteric Disease Case Interviewer**\
*Georgia Department of Public Health · Aug 2025–Present*
<details>
<summary> </summary>
* Conduct bilingual (English/Spanish) interviews for enteric disease investigations and outbreak tracing.
* Develop automated reporting tools in R to enhance data integrity and response times.
</details>

**Workforce Development Intern**\
*Council of State and Territorial Epidemiologists · Nov 2024–Present*
<details>
<summary> </summary>
* Lead data analysis and survey design to evaluate CDC grant impact on epidemiological workforce capacity.
* Authored continuing education materials on data privacy and parameterized reporting in R.
</details>

**Center for Urban Transformations Fellow**\
*Georgia State University · Jan 2025–Dec 2025*
<details>
<summary> </summary>
* Partnered with CHARRS to research air pollution exposure in West Atlanta communities.
* Composed MOUs to facilitate data sharing between CHARRS and multiple research universities.
* Designed and facilitated community data workshops to communicate findings.
</details>

**Emergency Medical Technician**\
*DeKalb County Fire Rescue Department · Jan 2023–Mar 2024*
<details>
<summary> </summary>
* Delivered emergency medical care and conducted patient assessments in high-stress environments.
* Applied NIMS/FEMA frameworks for multi-agency emergency response.
</details>

## Professional Affiliations & Community

**Professional Organizations:** Georgia Public Health Association (GPHA), Southern Alliance for Public Health Leadership

**Community:** Sacred Harp Singing Community

## Publications, Presentations & Posters

[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0003-2004-4203)

* **Harmon, J.** (2026). *[Insert Capstone Title]* (Master's capstone project). Georgia State University.
* **Harmon, J.**, et al. (2026). *[Poster Title]* (Poster presentation). Spenser Research Group, Atlanta, GA.
* **Harmon, J.**, et al. (2025). *[Poster Title]* (Poster presentation). PAR (Participatory Action Research) Group, Atlanta, GA.
* **Harmon, J.**, et al. (2025). *Title of Panel Discussion*. Panelist at [Event Name], Atlanta, GA.
